title: Do not ignore virtual interface member triggers
body:
Virtual interface member triggers used to not be added to the trigger vector computation, and instead they resolved to some random trigger bit. And the actual variables inserted in V3SchedVirIface for virtual interface member triggers were subsequently unused and removed.

Rewrote to what the likely intention was, but this brings back the did not converge issue reported in #5116 in two tests. Those test don't actually test the virtual interface member trigger, they happen to pass because the dependent combinational logic is resolved in ICO in one iteration which is always executed at least once on the initial iteration of ICO. (Also all combinational logic dependent on a virtual interface is always replicated into ICO, which I'm not sure is warranted, but it happens to make these tests "pass").

---

@wsnyder I suggest we merge this because the old version was completely broken. @YilouWang or one of @kbieganski's gang can then maybe look actually fixing #5116. We will need much more testing too, I'm not sure this whole business is sound.
